title: Ensure that Multi-Factor Authentication is 'Enabled' for All  Non-Service Accounts

input_row.title: Ensure that Multi-Factor Authentication is 'Enabled' for All  Non-Service Accounts
input_row.description: Setup multi-factor authentication for Google Cloud Platform accounts.
input_row.rationale: Multi-factor authentication requires more than one mechanism to authenticate a user. This secures user logins from attackers exploiting stolen or weak credentials.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console For each Google Cloud Platform project, folder, or organization: 1. Identify non-service accounts. 2. Manually verify that multi-factor authentication for each account is set.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console For each Google Cloud Platform project: 1. Identify non-service accounts. 2. Setup multi-factor authentication for each account.
input_row.default_value: By default, multi-factor authentication is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Navigate to the Google Cloud Console: Go to the IAM & Admin section for each Google Cloud project.
2. In the IAM section, identify non-service accounts by reviewing the list of accounts. Non-service accounts are typically user accounts that are not associated with service accounts.
3. For each non-service account, check if Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled. This can be done by selecting the account and reviewing the security settings or by looking for MFA options in the user account configuration.
4. Verify that MFA is configured. A secure configuration will have MFA enabled requiring a second form of authentication, such as a mobile device or security key.
5. Take note of any accounts that do not have MFA enabled to confirm compliance. If necessary, provide a report detailing accounts that need MFA setup or a summary of your findings.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:** Multi-Factor Authentication should be fully enabled for all non-service accounts.

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:** Compliance is confirmed if all non-service accounts are identified and MFA is enabled on each of those accounts. Any non-compliant account should be documented for remediation.
